rxn_class_summary: Photoelectrochemical asymmetric dehydrogenative [2+2] cycloaddition between alkyl 2-acyl imidazole ketones and alkenes, forging chiral cyclobutanes by activating two C(sp3)-H bonds and a C=C double bond under chiral-at-Rh Lewis acid / ferrocene-mediated electrolysis.
electrochemical_role: Ferrocene (Fc) acts as an anodic redox mediator: Fc is oxidized to Fc+ at the RVC anode, which then performs single-electron oxidation of the Rh-bound enolate intermediate II to a carbon radical cation III; the cathode reduces alcohol to H2 and alkoxide, completing the dehydrogenative cycle.

key_intermediates.name: Rh-bound alpha-carbonyl radical cation III (SET oxidation by Fc+)
Rh-bound allylic/alpha-carbonyl radical IV (after proton loss)
Rh-dienolate radical cation V (tautomer of VI after second SET)
Triplet 1,4-diradical VIII (after C-C bond formation with alkene)
Rh-coordinated cyclobutane IX (after ISC and ring closure)

electron_flow: At the anode, Fc is oxidized to Fc+, which performs SET on the Rh-enolate II to give radical cation III; deprotonation yields allylic radical IV, which is further oxidized (by Fc+) to V/VI, the Rh-bound enone. Visible light excites VI to triplet biradical VII, which adds to the alkene to give 1,4-diradical VIII, followed by ISC and C-C bond closure to cyclobutane IX; protons released are reduced at the Pt cathode to H2, achieving overall dehydrogenation.
rate_determining_step: Electrochemical dehydrogenative oxidation of the alkyl ketone to the enone intermediate VI (1 -> 52, ~10 h) is markedly slower than the subsequent photochemical [2+2] cycloaddition (52 -> 3, ~3 h).

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: TEMPO-adduct 53 isolated (8%, 23% e.e.) at the alpha-carbonyl position; cyclobutane formation suppressed, supporting radical intermediates III/IV.

probe_reagents.name: Phenyl vinyl ketone (Michael acceptor)
probe_reagents.observation: Gave Michael adduct 55 (28%, 96% e.e.) plus mono-addition 54, confirming the enolate intermediate II.

probe_reagents.name: Reaction without diene 2
probe_reagents.observation: Enone 52 isolated in 72% yield, confirming the dehydrogenative pathway generates VI before cycloaddition.

probe_reagents.name: Stoichiometric Fc+PF6- (no current)
probe_reagents.observation: With 10 equiv Fc+PF6-, product 3 obtained in 25% (97% e.e.) plus enone 52 (35%), demonstrating Fc+ is the kinetically competent oxidant.

probe_reagents.name: Light/current on-off (sequential)
probe_reagents.observation: Electrolysis without light gives only enone 52; subsequent irradiation without current converts 52 + diene to 3 (77%, 14:1 d.r., 96% e.e.), decoupling the EC dehydrogenation from the photo-[2+2] step.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of Fc / Fc + RhS-enolate
probe_reagents.observation: Catalytic current observed (oxidation peak enhanced, reduction peak vanishes) only when 1 + Rh + DABCO are present, confirming Fc+ oxidizes enolate II (Ep/2 ~0.50 V vs ~0.44 V for Fc+/Fc).

notes: Authors explicitly exclude an alternative pathway in which an electro-oxidized electron-rich olefin radical cation couples with another olefin: no cyclobutane forms in absence of diene, and the isolated enone 52 cleanly converts to product upon photolysis, supporting the 'dehydrogenation-then-photo-[2+2]' sequence over a radical-cation [2+2]. The chiral-at-Rh complex serves a dual role as Lewis acid and triplet photosensitizer, enabling enantiocontrol via the triplet biradical VII/VIII.
